fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing

diff --git a/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..06b33cb
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+/**
+ * NotificationAdapter interface — the contract all notification delivery
+ * channels must implement.
+ *
+ * Architecture: Event-driven via EventBus + Adapter pattern.
+ * The NotificationService listens to EventBus events, builds NotificationPayloads,
+ * and delegates delivery to the appropriate adapter based on channel.
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - Every adapter must implement this interface.
+ * - Adapters handle delivery only — they must NOT subscribe to EventBus directly.
+ * - The NotificationService is the sole coordinator between events and adapters.
+ * - Adapters must be stateless with respect to notification routing logic.
+ */
+
+import type { NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';
+
+export interface NotificationAdapter {
+  /** The channel this adapter handles */
+  readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+
+  /**
+   * Send a notification through this channel.
+   * Must return a NotificationResult indicating success or failure.
+   */
+  send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+}

- * 2. Event-driven notifications (services emit events, listeners handle side effects)
+ * 2. Event-driven notifications: services emit events via EventBus,
+ *    NotificationService subscribes and delegates to NotificationAdapter implementations
+ * 3. All notification channels implement the NotificationAdapter interface
  */
 
 // Models
@@ -44,6 +47,18 @@ export type {
   UserUpdatedEvent,
 } from './events/event-types.js';
 
+// Notification models

+} from './models/notification.js';

+
 // Services
 export { UserService } from './services/user.service.js';
 export { OrderService } from './services/order.service.js';

diff --git a/src/models/notification.ts b/src/models/notification.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c1ead1b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/models/notification.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,65 @@
+/**
+ * Notification model — core types for the notification system.
+ *
+ * Architecture: Event-driven via EventBus.
+ * All notifications originate from AppEvents processed by the NotificationService,
+ * which delegates delivery to NotificationAdapter implementations.
+ *
+ * Constraints:
+ * - All adapters must implement the NotificationAdapter interface.
+ * - All notifications must go through EventBus.emit() — never call adapters directly.
+ * - Each NotificationChannel maps to exactly one adapter.
+ */
+
+/**
+ * Supported notification channels.
+ * Add new channels here when implementing new adapters.
+ */
+export type NotificationChannel = 'email' | 'webhook' | 'in-app';
+
+/**
+ * Notification types that map to business events.
+ */
+export type NotificationType =
+  | 'order-confirmation'
+  | 'order-status-update'
+  | 'welcome'
+  | 'user-updated';
+
+/**
+ * The payload passed to notification adapters for delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationPayload {
+  /** Unique notification ID */
+  id: string;
+  /** The type of notification (maps to a business event) */
+  type: NotificationType;
+  /** The channel this notification will be delivered through */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Recipient identifier (email address, user ID, webhook URL, etc.) */
+  recipient: string;
+  /** Notification subject / title */
+  subject: string;
+  /** Notification body content */
+  body: string;
+  /** Additional metadata for adapter-specific behavior */
+  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+  /** When the notification was created */
+  createdAt: Date;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Result returned by adapters after attempting delivery.
+ */
+export interface NotificationResult {
+  /** Whether delivery succeeded */
+  success: boolean;
+  /** The notification that was sent */
+  notificationId: string;
+  /** The channel used for delivery */
+  channel: NotificationChannel;
+  /** Error message if delivery failed */
+  error?: string;
+  /** Timestamp of the delivery attempt */
+  deliveredAt: Date;
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tY7Lih.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: architecture choice (event-driven vs direct calls), core types, notification service, email adapter

alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log, inconsistent with how the rest of the codebase already works. Would require refactoring OrderService.createOrder to also use callbacks, increasing inconsistency.

constraints: 11 more developers will build on this foundation
Must define clear interfaces for adapter implementations
All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit()
parameters.context: The existing codebase has two competing patterns: EventBus (used for order:created) and CallbackRegistry (used for order:status-changed). The NotificationService mixes both. Need to pick one and unify.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is already the dominant pattern — OrderService, UserService both emit events through it. It provides: (1) loose coupling between producers and consumers, (2) an event log for debugging/testing, (3) typed events via TypeScript discriminated unions, (4) easy extensibility — new notification channels just subscribe to existing events. The CallbackRegistry pattern will be eliminated in favor of routing all events through EventBus.
parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture via EventBus, not direct calls

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture: All order events (creation, status changes) are emitted
 * via EventBus. The NotificationService subscribes to these events and
 * coordinates delivery through registered adapters.
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * TaskFlow Pro — A task and order management system.
 *
 * Architecture:
 * - Service Layer: UserService, OrderService, NotificationService (business logic)
 * - Repository Layer: UserRepository, OrderRepository (data access via Repository pattern)
 * - Adapter Layer: EmailAdapter (notification delivery channels)
 * - Utility Layer: Database, Logger, Pagination (infrastructure)
 * - Event System: EventBus with typed events (decoupled communication)
 *
 * Key architectural decisions:
 * 1. Repository pattern for all data access (no direct DB calls from services)
 * 2. Event-driven notifications: services emit events via EventBus,
 *    NotificationService subscribes and delegates to NotificationAdapter implementations
 * 3. All notification channels implement the NotificationAdapter interface
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * TaskFlow Pro — A task and order management system.
 *
 * Architecture:
 * - Service Layer: UserService, OrderService (business logic)
 * - Repository Layer: UserRepository, OrderRepository (data access via Repository pattern)
 * - Utility Layer: Database, Logger, Pagination (infrastructure)
 * - Event System: EventBus with typed events (decoupled communication)
 *
 * Key architectural decisions:
 * 1. Repository pattern for all data access (no direct DB calls from services)
 * 2. Event-driven notifications (services emit events, listeners handle side effects)
 */

parameters.detail:
Implemented:
1. ARCHITECTURE: Chose event-driven (EventBus) over direct calls. All notifications flow through EventBus.emit() → NotificationService → NotificationAdapter.
2. CORE TYPES: src/models/notification.ts — NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult.
3. ADAPTER INTERFACE: src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts — NotificationAdapter interface (channel, send method).
4. EMAIL ADAPTER: src/adapters/email.adapter.ts — EmailAdapter implements NotificationAdapter with in-memory stub.
5. NOTIFICATION SERVICE: Rewritten src/services/notification.service.ts — subscribes to EventBus, delegates to adapters via registerAdapter().
6. UNIFIED PATTERN: Removed CallbackRegistry usage from OrderService.updateOrderStatus — now emits via EventBus like all other events.
7. Updated tests and exports. All 69 tests pass, clean compile.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- All adapters MUST implement NotificationAdapter interface
- All notifications MUST go through EventBus.emit() — never call adapters directly
- Each NotificationChannel maps to exactly one adapter
- NotificationService is the sole coordinator between events and adapters
- Adapters handle delivery only — they must NOT subscribe to EventBus directly
parameters.summary: Agent 1: Notification system foundation complete — event-driven architecture via EventBus + NotificationAdapter pattern

parameters.command: git add src/adapters/notification.adapter.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/models/notification.ts src/index.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement event-driven notification system foundation

Unify notification architecture on EventBus pattern, replacing the mixed
EventBus/CallbackRegistry approach. All notifications now flow through
EventBus → NotificationService → NotificationAdapter implementations.

- Add NotificationAdapter interface, core types (NotificationPayload, etc.)
- Add EmailAdapter stub implementing NotificationAdapter
- Rewrite NotificationService to use adapter pattern with registerAdapter()
- Migrate OrderService.updateOrderStatus from CallbackRegistry to EventBus
- Update tests for new architecture (69/69 passing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
